wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for Seisamuse static site generator. Explain what Seisamuse is - a specialized CLI tool for academic researchers to convert Markdown content into professional academic websites. Cover installation prerequisites including Python 3.x, virtual environment setup, and dependency installation via requirements.txt. Document the basic workflow from content creation to site generation. Include step-by-step instructions for first-time users, showing how to run the build script, understand the generated site structure, and start a local development server. Provide practical examples of the simplest possible usage scenario to help users quickly understand the tool's purpose and capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontmatter Syntax and Metadata
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for frontmatter syntax and metadata fields used in Seisamuse content management. Explain the YAML frontmatter format with triple dashes (---) delimiters. Document all supported metadata fields including title (required), date (YYYY-MM-DD format), tags (comma-separated list), slug (URL-friendly version), excerpt (custom summary), and description (meta description). Provide detailed examples showing proper frontmatter formatting with practical code snippets from existing content files. Explain how frontmatter values are processed and used throughout the site generation pipeline. Include validation rules, formatting requirements, and common mistakes to avoid. Document the automatic slug generation from filenames and how dates affect chronological sorting.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSS Architecture and Variables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Seisamuse's CSS architecture and custom property system. Explain the CSS custom properties defined in :root (variables like --text, --bg, --accent, --muted, --border, --card-bg, --nav-bg, --code-bg, and --max-w) and how they enable theme customization. Document the CSS organization structure including resets, base styles, typography, layout, components, and responsive breakpoints. Detail the academic-focused design principles behind color choices, typography selection (Georgia, Palatino, system fonts), and spacing systems. Explain the CSS cascade order and how variables propagate throughout the stylesheet. Include practical examples of modifying color schemes by updating CSS variables and maintaining design consistency across light and dark modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Seisamuse build process. Explain the complete content loading pipeline including Markdown frontmatter extraction, metadata processing, and template rendering workflow. Document the five-phase build process: content loading, about page processing, homepage generation, blog listing creation, and individual post building. Detail the template context system, Jinja2 environment configuration, and file output structure. Include the command-line interface with build and serve commands, parameter handling, and error management. Provide examples of the build process execution, showing how posts are sorted, processed, and rendered into static HTML files. Address performance considerations, memory usage during large builds, and troubleshooting common build failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Inheritance Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the template inheritance pattern used in Seisamuse. Explain how base.html serves as the master template and the block system that enables content extension. Document the key blocks defined in base.html including title, description, and content blocks. Show how specialized templates extend the base template using Jinja2 inheritance syntax. Include practical examples of block overriding and variable substitution. Explain the inheritance chain and how child templates can override specific sections while maintaining overall structure. Address best practices for template organization and naming conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive project overview content explaining Seisamuse's architecture and purpose. Describe how it functions as a static site generator specifically designed for academic researchers and scientists. Explain the core workflow: Markdown content processing, frontmatter metadata extraction, Jinja2 template rendering, and static file generation. Detail the target audience (academic researchers, geoscience professionals) and key use cases (personal websites, research portfolios, academic blogging). Document the technical architecture including the build pipeline, template system, and content management approach. Explain the relationship between content files, templates, and generated output. Include the academic-focused design philosophy and how it differs from general-purpose static site generators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Post Organization and Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for organizing and structuring blog posts in Seisamuse. Explain the content/posts/ directory structure and filename requirements for automatic slug generation. Document the chronological sorting mechanism based on date metadata and how posts are displayed on the homepage and blog listing pages. Cover best practices for post organization including naming conventions, file placement, and directory management. Explain the automatic excerpt generation from post content and how excerpts are used in blog listings. Document reading time calculation methodology and its impact on post presentation. Include guidelines for managing multiple posts, archive organization, and maintaining chronological order across content updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Typography and Visual Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Seisamuse's typography system and visual design elements. Document the academic typography choices including serif fonts (Georgia, Palatino, Times New Roman) for headings and body text, and monospace fonts for code blocks. Explain font sizing scales, line heights, and spacing ratios used throughout the design. Detail the visual hierarchy with heading sizes (h1-h4), paragraph styling, and element-specific typography treatments. Cover the design system including accent colors, muted tones, borders, and background colors. Document the card-based design approach, navigation styling, and interactive elements. Include examples of how typography contributes to readability and academic presentation standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Seisamuse development workflow and local preview capabilities. Explain the serve command functionality, local HTTP server setup, and live preview features. Document the development cycle including content creation, incremental builds, and real-time preview testing. Cover the file watching mechanism, automatic rebuild triggers, and development server configuration. Include best practices for content authoring, template customization during development, and debugging techniques. Provide guidance on optimizing the development experience, managing content drafts, and testing different themes or layouts. Address common development issues, server port conflicts, and hot-reload limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page-Specific Templates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Seisamuse's page-specific templates and their unique purposes. Detail the index.html template for the homepage, blog.html for blog listings, post.html for individual blog posts, about.html for the about page, and links.html for resource links. Explain the context variables available to each template type including active navigation state, content data structures, and pagination variables. Document the template rendering process for each page type and how they utilize the base template. Include examples of template-specific logic, conditional rendering, and data manipulation. Address template customization for each page type and common modification patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content management documentation covering all aspects of creating and organizing content for Seisamuse. Explain the content directory structure with content/posts/ for blog posts and content/about.md for the about page. Document frontmatter syntax and metadata fields including title, date, tags, slug, excerpt, and description. Provide comprehensive examples of frontmatter usage with practical code snippets. Explain the automatic excerpt generation, reading time calculation, and slug creation from filenames. Cover best practices for writing academic content, including formatting guidelines, image embedding, and cross-references. Document the content loading process and how posts are sorted chronologically. Include troubleshooting tips for common content-related issues and validation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: About Page Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for managing the about page content in Seisamuse. Explain the content/about.md file structure and how it integrates with the about template system. Document the markdown formatting requirements and recommended sections including personal introduction, research interests, education background, selected publications, and contact information. Provide guidelines for updating biographical information, research focus areas, and professional links. Explain how the about page is rendered using the about.html template and integrated into the overall site navigation. Include best practices for maintaining professional academic profiles and ensuring content consistency across different sections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive Design and Dark Mode
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Seisamuse's responsive design implementation and dark mode support. Explain the mobile-first approach with breakpoints at 640px and how the design adapts for different screen sizes. Document the navigation system including the mobile hamburger menu, responsive grid layouts, and flexible components. Detail the dark mode implementation using @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) that automatically switches color variables for reduced eye strain and battery optimization. Explain the backdrop-filter effect on navigation, sticky positioning, and responsive typography scaling. Include practical examples of testing responsive behavior and customizing breakpoints for specific device requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation covering all supported hosting platforms and deployment methods. Document static hosting deployment for GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, and traditional web hosts. Explain the site directory structure, file organization, and asset delivery requirements. Cover continuous integration setup with automated builds and deployments, including GitHub Actions workflows and deployment triggers. Detail CDN integration, asset optimization, and performance best practices for production sites. Include version control integration, branch-based deployments, and rollback procedures. Address security considerations, SSL certificate management, and domain configuration. Provide platform-specific setup guides with complete configuration examples and troubleshooting steps for common deployment issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation and Context Variables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the navigation system and context variables in Seisamuse templates. Document the active navigation state management using the 'active' context variable and how it controls menu highlighting. Explain the 'root' context variable for URL path resolution and its role in maintaining relative links. Detail the 'year' context variable for copyright information and how it's automatically populated. Document the template context structure including available content data, metadata variables, and utility functions. Explain conditional logic usage for navigation items and how templates handle different content types. Include examples of context variable usage and common patterns for dynamic content rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive template system documentation explaining Seisamuse's Jinja2-based templating architecture. Detail the template inheritance pattern starting with base.html and how specialized templates extend it. Document the template context variables available to each page type including active navigation, site metadata, and content data. Explain the template rendering process and how content is passed from the build script to templates. Cover the responsive design implementation and CSS integration. Document template customization techniques, variable usage, and conditional logic. Include practical examples of modifying existing templates and creating new ones. Address template debugging, error handling, and performance considerations. Provide guidelines for maintaining template consistency across the site.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Best Practices and Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content best practices documentation for creating effective academic content with Seisamuse. Cover markdown formatting guidelines specific to academic writing including headings, lists, tables, and mathematical notation. Document image embedding best practices, cross-reference formatting, and link management. Explain academic writing standards appropriate for seismology and geophysics content. Provide guidelines for structuring research-focused blog posts, including methodology descriptions, results presentation, and conclusion formatting. Document accessibility considerations for academic content and inclusive writing practices. Include troubleshooting guidance for common content issues, validation requirements, and quality assurance checks before site generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Styling Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for styling individual components and layout elements in Seisamuse. Document the styling of key components including navigation (.nav-links, .site-title), hero section (.hero, .avatar), blog post cards (.post-list, .post-item), link cards (.link-grid, .link-card), and footer elements. Explain the styling patterns used for interactive states (hover effects, active states), form elements, buttons (.btn), and content areas. Detail the container system (.container) and layout patterns (flexbox, grid). Include examples of component customization, extending existing styles, and creating new component variations while maintaining design consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Customization Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a practical template customization guide for modifying Seisamuse templates. Document safe modification practices and common customization scenarios including adding new sections, modifying layout elements, and changing visual styling. Explain how to add new template variables and modify existing ones. Provide step-by-step examples for popular customizations like adding social media links, implementing analytics tracking, or modifying the header layout. Document template debugging techniques and error handling strategies. Include guidelines for maintaining template compatibility during updates and best practices for template organization. Address common pitfalls and provide troubleshooting guidance for template-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed styling and theming documentation covering Seisamuse's CSS architecture and customization options. Explain the academic-focused design principles and typography choices. Document the CSS custom properties system and how to modify colors, spacing, and layout variables. Cover the responsive design implementation including mobile-first approach and breakpoint handling. Detail the dark mode support and how to customize color schemes. Explain the CSS organization, class naming conventions, and styling best practices. Include practical examples of common customizations like changing accent colors, adjusting typography, and modifying layout elements. Address browser compatibility, performance optimization, and accessibility considerations in custom themes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation covering the complete workflow from content creation to production deployment. Document the build process including content loading, template rendering, and static file generation. Explain the command-line interface with build and serve commands, including development server functionality and live preview capabilities. Cover the site generation process, file output structure, and directory organization. Document deployment strategies for various hosting platforms including GitHub Pages, Netlify, and traditional web hosts. Include continuous integration setup, automated deployment workflows, and version control best practices. Address performance optimization, asset compression, and CDN integration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common build and deployment issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation addressing common issues and their solutions. Cover installation problems including Python version conflicts, dependency resolution errors, and virtual environment setup issues. Document build-time errors such as missing dependencies, template rendering failures, and content processing errors. Address runtime issues including server startup problems, file permission errors, and output generation failures. Include debugging techniques for template issues, CSS problems, and content formatting errors. Provide solutions for deployment challenges and hosting-specific issues. Document performance optimization tips and memory usage considerations. Include frequently asked questions about customization limits, feature availability, and migration scenarios. Organize solutions by error type and provide step-by-step resolution guides.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Seisamuse: Academic Personal Website Generator

## Project Introduction

**Seisamuse** is a lightweight, static site generator designed specifically for academic researchers and scientists. The project serves as a personal website platform that converts Markdown content into a clean, professional academic website with minimal dependencies and maximum flexibility.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal of Seisamuse is to provide researchers with a streamlined solution for creating professional academic websites without the complexity of heavy CMS platforms. It focuses on content-first publishing with elegant typography and responsive design.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Content-Centric Publishing**: Prioritize Markdown-based content creation over complex website management
- **Academic Professionalism**: Deliver clean, scholarly aesthetics suitable for research institutions
- **Technical Simplicity**: Maintain minimal dependencies while offering powerful features
- **Research Integration**: Support scientific workflows with proper citation handling and technical formatting

### Target Audience
- Academic researchers in seismology, geophysics, and related earth sciences
- Scientists requiring professional online presence for research portfolios
- Researchers seeking lightweight alternatives to WordPress or other CMS platforms

## Technical Architecture

Seisamuse follows a modular, content-driven architecture built around three core pillars:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Markdown Content] --> B[Frontmatter Parser]
    B --> C[Markdown Processor]
    C --> D[Jinja2 Templates]
    D --> E[Static HTML Output]
    
    F[Build Script] --> G[File System Loader]
    G --> H[Template Environment]
    H --> I[Site Generator]
    I --> E
    
    J[Requirements] --> K[Jinja2]
    J --> L[Markdown]
    J --> M[Python-Frontmatter]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Content Management Layer**
- Markdown-based content with YAML frontmatter metadata
- Automatic post processing with reading time estimation
- Hierarchical content organization (posts, about, links)

**Template Engine**
- Jinja2 templating system for consistent layout rendering
- Base template inheritance for maintainable CSS/HTML structure
- Dynamic navigation and active state management

**Build Pipeline**
- Sequential processing: content loading → template rendering → file generation
- Local development server with hot-reload capabilities
- Optimized static asset generation

### Design Patterns

**Static Site Generation Pattern**
- Pre-rendered HTML files for optimal performance
- No server-side processing required
- CDN-friendly architecture

**Template Inheritance Pattern**
- Base template with block placeholders
- Reusable components across different page types
- Consistent styling and navigation structure

**Configuration-Driven Architecture**
- Centralized configuration in build script
- Environment-specific settings
- Extensible template system

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Build Script (`build.py`)**
The central orchestrator that manages the entire site generation process. It handles content loading, template processing, and file output generation. The script provides both build-only functionality and integrated development server capabilities.

**Template System (`templates/`)**
A collection of Jinja2 templates that define the site's structural foundation. The system uses template inheritance to maintain consistency across different page types while allowing for specialized layouts.

### Core Modules

**Content Processing Engine**
- Frontmatter parsing for metadata extraction
- Markdown conversion with syntax highlighting and table of contents
- Automatic excerpt generation and reading time calculation
- Date parsing and sorting mechanisms

**Template Rendering System**
- Base template with navigation and footer components
- Specialized templates for different content types
- Responsive design with CSS custom properties
- Mobile-first approach with progressive enhancement

**Static Asset Management**
- Minimal CSS framework with academic color schemes
- Responsive typography optimized for readability
- Dark mode support with system preference detection
- Performance-optimized layout system

### Configuration Approach

**Centralized Settings**
The build script maintains all configuration in a single location, making it easy to customize site-wide parameters like titles, author information, and feature toggles.

**Environment Configuration**
- Development vs production optimizations
- Template caching strategies
- File system permissions and security considerations

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies (`requirements.txt`)**
- Jinja2: Templating engine for dynamic content rendering
- Markdown: Text markup processing with advanced extensions
- Python-Frontmatter: Metadata extraction from Markdown files

**Optional Dependencies**
- HTTP server for local development preview
- File system utilities for asset optimization

### Integration Points

**Content Integration**
- Seamless Markdown workflow integration
- Frontmatter metadata support for rich content
- Automatic content discovery and processing

**Development Workflow**
- Local preview server with automatic refresh
- File watching for development efficiency
- Cross-platform compatibility

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Content Files] --> B[Build Process]
    B --> C[Template Engine]
    C --> D[Static Output]
    
    E[Development Server] --> F[Live Preview]
    F --> G[Browser Refresh]
    
    H[Dependencies] --> I[Jinja2]
    H --> J[Markdown]
    H --> K[Frontmatter]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Academic-Grade Typography**
- Serif fonts optimized for long-form reading
- Proper spacing and line-height calculations
- Academic color schemes with accessible contrast ratios

**Research-Focused Features**
- Publication-style formatting for scientific content
- Citation-friendly markup handling
- Code block syntax highlighting for technical examples

**Professional Presentation**
- Clean, minimalist design suitable for academic institutions
- Responsive layout for mobile and desktop viewing
- Dark mode support for extended reading sessions

### Implementation Highlights

**Content Management (`content/`)**
- Structured content organization with clear separation of concerns
- Automatic metadata extraction and processing
- Flexible post categorization and tagging system

**Performance Optimization**
- Static site generation eliminates server overhead
- Minimal JavaScript footprint for fast loading
- Optimized CSS delivery with critical path styling

**Developer Experience**
- Simple command-line interface for building and serving
- Hot-reload development server for efficient iteration
- Clear error messages and debugging information

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ContentLoading
    ContentLoading --> TemplateProcessing
    TemplateProcessing --> FileGeneration
    FileGeneration --> [*]
    
    ContentLoading --> ErrorHandling: Invalid Content
    ErrorHandling --> ContentLoading: Fix Issues
```

**Content Processing Pipeline**
- Multi-stage content validation and transformation
- Automatic metadata extraction and enrichment
- Error handling with graceful degradation

**Template Rendering System**
- Hierarchical template inheritance for maintainability
- Context-aware rendering with conditional logic
- Asset optimization during build process

**Deployment Architecture**
- Static file generation for universal compatibility
- CDN-ready structure for global distribution
- Minimal hosting requirements for cost-effective deployment

---

**Sources:**
- [build.py](build.py)
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt)
- [content/about.md](content/about.md)
- [content/posts/welcome-to-seisamuse.md](content/posts/welcome-to-seisamuse.md)
- [templates/base.html](templates/base.html)
- [templates/index.html](templates/index.html)
- [site/css/style.css](site/css/style.css)
- [templates/blog.html](templates/blog.html)
- [content/posts/environmental-seismology-intro.md](content/posts/environmental-seismology-intro.md)

</blog>
